name: Acanthisittidae
description: New Zealand wrens are tiny, insectivorous passerines endemic to New Zealand. They are the most basal living passerine lineage, representing the sister group to all other songbirds.

children.name: Acanthisitta
children.description: Acanthisitta is a genus of New Zealand wrens containing the rifleman, one of New Zealand's smallest birds.

children.name: Acanthisitta chloris
children.description: The rifleman is New Zealand's smallest bird species, measuring just 8 cm in length. It is named for its rifle-green male plumage and is one of only two surviving New Zealand wren species.

children.name: Xenicus
children.description: Xenicus is a genus of New Zealand wrens that includes both living and recently extinct species.

children.name: Xenicus gilviventris
children.description: The South Island wren is a small alpine passerine found only in the mountains of New Zealand's South Island. It is one of only two surviving New Zealand wren species.

children.name: Xenicus longipes
children.description: The bush wren was a small, flight-challenged New Zealand endemic that inhabited native forests. It went extinct in the late 20th century due to introduced mammalian predators.

children.name: Traversia
children.description: Traversia is a monotypic genus of flightless New Zealand wren, now extinct.

children.name: Traversia lyalli
children.description: The Stephens Island wren was a flightless passerine endemic to Stephens Island, New Zealand. It was famously driven to extinction shortly after 1895 by a single lighthouse keeper's cat.
